stint_ids: Newton, Francis J___Cape of Good Hope___1883
persons.arguments_against: Six stints assigned to one man. The Cape 1883 ADC/private-secretary stint matches the entry verbatim (extra A.D.C. to the Cape governor, then private secretary) with colony and tenure agreeing. The British Honduras colonial-secretaryship and the Treasurer stints across Rhodesia / Southern Rhodesia / Swaziland / South Africa all carry initials F.J. and the distinctive honour set C.M.G. + C.V.O.(1911) + K.C.M.G. — the Treasurer role recorded under the evolving Southern-African territory labels. A different F.J. Newton holding the C.V.O. and serving as Treasurer in Southern Africa is not credible.

evidence_cited.stint_id: Newton, Francis J___Cape of Good Hope___1883

persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person serving the same colony in the same years and offices, or a printed birth/death date excluding these years.

notes: production high-value slice batch 2, hand-adjudicated (Claude Code, in-session)
